case.question: Which single final-build priority should Team Spiral choose to maximize its chance of winning? Follow the latest valid judging guidance. Answer POLISH or PROVE.

decision_slots.description: the centerpiece shown to hackathon judges

initial_evidence.text: Team Spiral must choose exactly one final-build priority for its hackathon submission.

Decision rule: follow the latest valid judging weights. If one criterion is dominant, optimize for that criterion; if Technological Implementation, Design, Potential Impact, and Quality of the Idea are equally weighted, prioritize an end-to-end proof that demonstrates all four in one flow.

Current internal brief: Design is the dominant judging criterion, so spend the remaining build time on additional UI polish and make polished screens the demo centerpiece. This brief remains operative unless a later correction explicitly supersedes it.
The screens are already judge-readable; a live before-to-after reasoning diff is the missing element needed for an end-to-end proof.
The submission video must remain a narrated three-minute demo regardless of which final-build priority is chosen.

late_evidence.text: Latest judging correction: Technological Implementation, Design, Potential Impact, and Quality of the Idea are equally weighted. This guidance explicitly supersedes evidence R3; use the existing readable UI and prioritize an end-to-end proof with a live reasoning diff.

evidence_bindings.public_summary: The currently operative design-dominant brief supplies the legacy judging premise.
The latest equal-weight judging guidance supplies the replacement premise.
The narrated three-minute video constraint remains stable across the revision.

transition_bindings.public_name: Write current judging premise into priority state
Reconcile latest judging guidance with prior priority

claim_boundary: This is a synthetic English product-walkthrough case, not a causal benchmark or fresh quality evaluation.
Evidence R3 is valid within the pre-event horizon and becomes invalid only after R6 explicitly supersedes it.
The premise/decision/stable program, oracle evidence values, event scope, operator order, and terminal surrogate target are explicitly supplied rather than discovered.
The fixed v0.5.1 temporal topology is reused only as a public mechanism scaffold for this hackathon strategy scenario.
No field from the separate gold artifact, downstream grade, provider output, or private reasoning enters the controller or provider request; the surrogate target remains an explicit oracle input.
A before-to-after output change cannot isolate the effect of the control map because the visible evidence horizon and revision envelope also change.
